name: Sage Holloway
summary: 6+ years of conservation field work across BLM and USFS lands across the Intermountain West, with deep experience in rangeland health assessment for BLM and USFS partners. Comfortable spike-camping for extended hitches, navigating off-trail, and running data collection to agency protocol.

experience.title: Crew Lead
bullets: Maintained 14 miles of backcountry trail with hand crew; packed in via raft
Collected vegetation and soil data on 20 AIM plots in the Uinta Mountains for BLM monitoring
Spike-camped for 3-day hitches in the Great Basin; carried 75lb pack in remote terrain
Wrote daily field notes and uploaded data nightly via Survey123; zero missing records over 3 weeks

experience.title: Field Technician II
bullets: Operated chainsaw and hand tools to remove Russian knapweed from 120 acres of pinyon-juniper woodland
Wrote daily field notes and uploaded data nightly via AGOL; zero missing records over 8 weeks
Logged 3 wildlife observations into iNaturalist; supported mule deer habitat assessment
Restored 6 miles of riparian corridor via willow staking and bank stabilization

experience.title: AmeriCorps Member
bullets: Spike-camped for 3-day hitches in the Henry Mountains; carried 55lb pack in remote terrain
Mapped 14 miles of fenceline using Trimble GPS; submetric accuracy
Collected vegetation and soil data on 5 AIM plots in the West Desert for BLM monitoring
Coordinated with BLM biologist on pinyon-juniper removal surveys across 1200 acres
